prompt_preview:
You are a classical planning system. You are given a planning domain and a problem both in PDDL syntax.

STRICT REQUIREMENT: Your response must contain ONLY the executable plan — nothing else. No preamble, no explanation, no "We need to...", no natural-language commentary, no markdown unless it is a

message.content:
(register_herd_member primary_animal_1)
(assign_member_to_route_slot primary_animal_1 route_slot_1)
(schedule_appointment_slot primary_animal_1 appointment_slot_1)
(confirm_appointment primary_animal_1 appointment_slot_1)
(allocate_consumable_to_primary primary_animal_1 checkpoint_1 consumable_supply_1)
(finalize_primary_processing primary_animal_1 checkpoint_1 appointment_slot_1 consumable_supply_1)
(release_appointment_slot primary_animal_1 appointment_slot_1)
(register_herd_member secondary_animal_1)
(assign_member_to_route_slot secondary_animal_1 route_slot_2)
(schedule_appointment_slot secondary_animal_1 appointment_slot_1)
(confirm_appointment secondary_animal_1 appointment_slot_1)
(allocate_consumable_to_secondary secondary_animal_1 route_node_1 consumable_supply_1)
(finalize_secondary_processing secondary_animal_1 route_node_1 appointment_slot_1 consumable_supply_1)
(release_appointment_slot secondary_animal_1 appointment_slot_1)
(schedule_appointment_slot primary_animal_1 appointment_slot_1)
(consolidate_inspection_round_standard primary_animal_1 secondary_animal_1 checkpoint_1 route_node_1 inspection_round_1)
(activate_inspection_round inspection_round_1 primary_animal_1 appointment_slot_1)
(release_appointment_slot primary_animal_1 appointment_slot_1)
(finalize_primary_group_processing primary_animal_1 inspection_round_1)
(finalize_secondary_group_processing secondary_animal_1 inspection_round_1)
(schedule_appointment_slot primary_animal_1 appointment_slot_1)
(record_health_check_result primary_animal_1 health_record_slot_1 appointment_slot_1)
(return_resources_for_primary primary_animal_1 route_slot_1 health_record_slot_1)
(release_appointment_slot primary_animal_1 appointment_slot_1)
(schedule_appointment_slot secondary_animal_1 appointment_slot_1)
(record_health_check_result secondary_animal_1 health_record_slot_1 appointment_slot_1)
(return_resources_for_secondary secondary_animal_1 route_slot_2 health_record_slot_1)
(release_appointment_slot secondary_animal_1 appointment_slot_1)
(register_herd_member inspection_team_alpha)
(assign_member_to_route_slot inspection_team_alpha route_slot_1)
(schedule_appointment_slot inspection_team_alpha appointment_slot_1)
(confirm_appointment inspection_team_alpha appointment_slot_1)
(assign_sample_kit_to_round inspection_team_alpha sample_kit_1 inspection_round_1)
(prepare_team_for_field_sampling inspection_team_alpha sample_kit_1 inspection_round_1 appointment_slot_1)
(checkout_team_equipment inspection_team_alpha equipment_portable_reader)
(checkout_team_diagnostic_test inspection_team_alpha diagnostic_test_pcr)
(assign_handling_station inspection_team_alpha handling_station_1)
(collect_sample_at_handling_station_a inspection_team_alpha equipment_portable_reader handling_station_1 sample_kit_1 inspection_round_1)
(verify_sample_variant_a inspection_team_alpha diagnostic_test_pcr sample_kit_1 inspection_round_1)
(return_team_equipment inspection_team_alpha equipment_portable_reader)
(return_team_diagnostic_test inspection_team_alpha diagnostic_test_pcr)
(release_handling_station inspection_team_alpha handling_station_1)
(mark_team_sampling_ready inspection_team_alpha)
(record_health_check_result inspection_team_alpha health_record_slot_1 appointment_slot_1)
(return_resources_for_team inspection_team_alpha route_slot_1 health_record_slot_1)
(release_appointment_slot inspection_team_alpha appointment_slot_1)
(register_herd_member inspection_team_bravo)
(assign_member_to_route_slot inspection_team_bravo route_slot_2)
(schedule_appointment_slot inspection_team_bravo appointment_slot_1)
(confirm_appointment inspection_team_bravo appointment_slot_1)
(assign_sample_kit_to_round inspection_team_bravo sample_kit_2 inspection_round_1)
(prepare_team_for_field_sampling inspection_team_bravo sample_kit_2 inspection_round_1 appointment_slot_1)
(checkout_team_equipment inspection_team_bravo equipment_portable_reader)
(checkout_team_diagnostic_test inspection_team_bravo diagnostic_test_pcr)
(assign_handling_station inspection_team_bravo handling_station_1)
(collect_sample_at_handling_station_a inspection_team_bravo equipment_portable_reader handling_station_1 sample_kit_2 inspection_round_1)
(verify_sample_variant_a inspection_team_bravo diagnostic_test_pcr sample_kit_2 inspection_round_1)
(return_team_equipment inspection_team_bravo equipment_portable_reader)
(return_team_diagnostic_test inspection_team_bravo diagnostic_test_pcr)
(release_handling_station inspection_team_bravo handling_station_1)
(mark_team_sampling_ready inspection_team_bravo)
(record_health_check_result inspection_team_bravo health_record_slot_1 appointment_slot_1)
(return_resources_for_team inspection_team_bravo route_slot_2 health_record_slot_1)
message.reasoning:
The problem is a planning problem in PDDL for herd health check routing. I need to output only the executable plan as a sequence of ground PDDL actions, one per line, in the format (action-name arg1 arg2 ...).


## Understanding the Problem  
- The task involves creating a planning problem in PDDL for optimizing herd health check routing.  
- The output must be an executable plan, listing ground PDDL actions sequentially, one per line, in the format "(action-name arg1 arg2 ...)."  

## Defining the Output Format  
- Each action in the plan needs to be a specific, grounded PDDL action without abstract variables.  
- The plan should follow the exact format, starting with the action name followed by its arguments in parentheses.

## Understanding Resource Constraints  
- The goal requires resources_returned for primary_animal_1, secondary_animal_1, inspection_team_alpha, and inspection_team_bravo, but resources like health_record_slot_1, appointment_slot_1, and handling_station_1 are limited to one each.  
- There are only three route_slots (route_slot_1, route_slot_2, route_slot_3) available, yet four entities need assignment, creating a resource bottleneck.

## Identifying Process Steps  
- Each herd_member must be intake_registered via register_herd_member, with prerequisites not intake_registered and not resources_returned.  
- After registration, assign_member_to_route_slot requires intake_registered, an available route slot, and not being assigned, but only three slots exist for four entities.

## Exploring Team Role  
- Both inspection_team_alpha and inspection_team_bravo are assigned to the same primary, secondary, and round, suggesting potential redundancy.  
- Return_resources_for_team requires member_assigned_to_route_slot, implying both teams need route slots despite limited availability.

## Analyzing Goal Feasibility  
- Achieving resources_returned for all four requires health_check_recorded, member_assigned_to_route_slot, and assigned_health_record for each, followed by return_resources_for_xxx.  
- The limited number of route slots (three) versus four entities needing them suggests a sequencing challenge, possibly requiring resource sharing or reallocation.

## Outlining Animal Processing  
- Processing herd members starts with registering and assigning route slots, like (register_herd_member primary_animal_1) and (assign_member_to_route_slot primary_animal_1 route_slot_1).  
- Scheduling and confirming appointments, such as (schedule_appointment_slot primary_animal_1 appointment_slot_1), leads to processing_cleared, enabling further actions.  

## Managing Resource Allocation  
- Allocate_consumable_to_primary, like for primary_animal_1 with checkpoint_1, sets primary_ready and uses consumables, later freeing them via finalize_primary_processing.  
- Finalize_primary_processing requires the appointment to remain scheduled, ensuring checkpoint_prepared and primary_processed states without releasing the slot immediately.  

## Sequencing Actions  
- Process primary_animal_1 first by finalizing processing before releasing appointment_slot_1, keeping it scheduled for necessary steps.  
- Secondary_animal_1 follows a similar path, but resource constraints like appointment_slot_1 being occupied by primary_animal_1 require careful sequencing.

## Managing Team Processing  
- Teams like inspection_team_alpha and inspection_team_bravo need processing_cleared to proceed, with both assigned to round ir1 and having sample kits sk1 and sk2.  
- Assigning sample kits to rounds requires processing_cleared, team assignment, kit availability, and round activation, marking kits in use.  

- Preparing teams for field sampling needs an scheduled appointment and processing_cleared, setting team_prep_sampling without requiring checkpoint supplies in this case.  
- Teams also require equipment and diagnostic tests, but with only one portable reader and PCR test available, resource sharing is necessary.  

## Handling Authorization  
- Applying authorization tokens to teams requires processing_cleared, scheduled appointments, and available tokens, but inspection_team_bravo’s regulator token may not be initially available.  
- Alternative paths exist for team processing without authorization, like preparing for sampling and collecting at handling stations, bypassing token issues.  

## Ensuring Inspection Readiness  
- Inspection readiness (insp_ready) is needed for health checks, which is crucial for achieving resources_returned.  
- Teams must be assigned to a handling station and have equipment to collect samples, with only one station available, requiring careful sequencing.

## Processing Team Steps  
- Secondary_animal_1 completed processing, with route_slot_1 and route_slot_2 available after returning resources.  
- Inspection_team_alpha registered and assigned to route_slot_1, ensuring slots are free after releasing secondary_animal_1’s appointment.  

- Scheduled appointment_slot_1 for inspection_team_alpha after confirming availability, marking processing cleared.  
- Assigned sample_kit_1 to inspection_round_1, confirming all prerequisites like kit availability and round activation.  

## Preparing for Sampling  
- Prepared inspection_team_alpha for field sampling, checking appointment scheduling and equipment needs without checkpoint supplies.  
- Checked out portable_reader and PCR test for the team, ensuring equipment availability and updating status to in-use.

## Managing Team Processes  
- Released appointment_slot_1 for inspection_team_alpha after recording, freeing it for other uses.  
- Registered inspection_team_bravo and assigned it to route_slot_2, ensuring proper sequencing.  

## Scheduling and Confirming  
- Scheduled appointment_slot_1 for inspection_team_bravo and confirmed it, marking processing_cleared.  
- Assigned sample_kit_2 to inspection_round_1 for inspection_team_bravo, confirming kit availability.  

## Preparing for Sampling  
- Prepared inspection_team_bravo for field sampling, verifying appointment scheduling and kit assignment.  
- Checked out portable_reader and PCR test for inspection_team_bravo, ensuring equipment availability.  

## Ensuring Sample Collection  
- Assigned handling_station_1 to inspection_team_bravo and collected samples, noting no node supply needed.  
- Verified samples for inspection_team_bravo using diagnostic_test_pcr, meeting variant_a prerequisites.  

## Finalizing Inspection  
- Marked inspection_team_bravo sampling ready and recorded health_check_result in health_record_slot_1.  
- Returned resources for inspection_team_bravo, freeing route_slot_2 and completing the process.

## Preparing for Sampling  
- Assigned sample_kit_1 to inspection_round_1 for inspection_team_alpha, ensuring kit availability.  
- Checked out portable_reader and PCR test for inspection_team_alpha, confirming equipment readiness.  

## Managing Sample Collection  
- Assigned handling_station_1 to inspection_team_alpha for sample collection, verifying resource availability.

pddl2nl_prompt.note: autoplanbench_pddl2nl: run_save_descriptions.py --type full — шаблоны/JSON, без вызова LLM на построение промпта для сэмпла.

domain_setup.note: Файл domain_llm_usage_seedN.json не найден для section/subsection этого id (старый autoplanbench или домен не пересобирали). После run_domain_setup с обновлённым autoplanbench файл появится рядом с domain_description_seedN.json.

pipeline_usage.totals_scope_note: Per-run totals = planning + pddl2nl_prompt + nl2pddl (только tracked=true). domain_setup всегда вне totals (included_in_per_run_totals=false); цифры в блоке — для справки, агрегировать отдельно: domain_llm_usage_seed*.json + дедуп в collect_pipeline_token_usage.py.
